State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

July 22, 1983

Sidney Maleck
City Attorney
11333 Valley Blvd.
El Monte, CA  91734

Re:  Your Request for Advice, Our
     Advice No. A-83-171

Dear Mr. Maleck:

     Your letter requests advice on behalf of the City Council of
El Monte with respect to certain past conduct of El Monte's
mayor, Thomas Keiser, and the ongoing conduct of Planning
Commissioner Michael Hamud.  We cannot issue advice on
Mayor Keiser's or Commissioner Hamud's past conduct.1/  We
will refer your letter to our Enforcement Division for their
review.2/

     Should you have further questions regarding pending actions,
please do not hesitate to have the officials involved contact us
for advice.  I may be reached at 916/322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Robert E. Leidign
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

REL:km
cc: Mayor Thomas Keiser
    Enclosure
    cc: FPPC Enforcement Division

_________________________

1/  The Commission's policy has always been that we should
not give advice regarding past conduct which may or may not have
violated the provisions of the Political Reform Act.

2/  Our statement here does not indicate any opinion
either way as to whether the conduct discussed in your letter
would or would not be found to be a violation of the Act.

CITY OF EL MONTE

CITY HALL • 11333 VALLEY BOULEVARD
EL MONTE, CALIFORNIA 91734
TELEPHONE (213) 575-2250

July 8, 1983

CITY ATTORNEY
SIDNEY MALECK

Ms. Barbara Milman,
Chief of Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms. Milman:

I have had a number of conversations with Mr. Robert Leidigh of your office regarding a possible conflict of interest that may exist in our city.

Because of the complexity of the fact situation, Mr. Leidigh requested that I set forth the facts in a letter along with the correspondence that is attached and submit the entire matter for review and a formal opinion by the FPPC.

The letter and attachments are enclosed.

If you have any further questions, please advise.

Very truly yours,

Sidney Maleck
City Attorney

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance
(916) 322-5662

Administration
322-5660

322-5901

Enforcement
322-6441

Statements and Economic Interests
322-6444

July 13, 1983


Sidney Maleck
City Attorney
11333 Valley Blvd.
El Monte, CA  91734

Re:  A-83-171

Dear Mr. Maleck:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Robert E. Leigh, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief, Legal Division

BAM:plh
Enclosure

July 8, 1983

Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804
Attention:  Ms. Barbara Milman
             Chief of Legal Division

Dear Ms. Milman:

At a regular meeting of the City Council of the City of El Monte, California, my office was directed by the City Council to obtain an opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission as to the propriety of:

     1.  An appointment made by the Mayor of our City, Mr. Thomas Keiser (who is an attorney) of another attorney, Mr. Michael Hamud, to this City's Planning Commission; and

     2.  The continued prosecution of cases by Mr. Hamud, who is now a planning commissioner, against the City and individual police officers of the City.

In order for the Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) personnel to render an opinion, the following background facts are submitted which should help to clarify the relationship between the parties.

FACTS

In April of 1982 Mayor Keiser of our City was elected by a vote of the people.  This was the first popular election for mayor held in the City since its inception.

Since El Monte is a general law city all prior mayors had been elected by the City Council.  In November of 1981 the people of the City changed this by voting in favor of popular election.

The Mayor had served two consecutive prior terms as a City Councilman.  Each term was for a four-year period.  Running in opposition to the present Mayor was another City Councilman by the name of Jack Crippen who had served three consecutive four-year terms and had been elected by the City Council for eight of the twelve years to be Mayor of the City.

Visit Friendly El Monte

Ms. Milman
Page 2


Three days (3) before the election a paper was published by the
supporters of Mayor Keiser alleging facts which are in dispute in
a lawsuit and circulated to the voters of the City. The paper not
only made certain allegations against Councilman Crippen but named
a number of other prominent individuals in the community and also
made allegations about them individually and jointly with respect
to their relationship with Councilman Crippen who was running for
Mayor.

As a result of those allegations Councilman Crippen, who was
defeated by Mayor Keiser, and two other persons, both former
Mayors of the City of El Monte, Mr. Elmer Geronsin and Mr. Dale
Ingram, have instituted defamation actions against Mayor Keiser.

Mayor Keiser was strongly supported in his election by a local
attorney by the name of Michael Hamud. In April of 1982 Mayor
Keiser was elected. Prior to September 7, 1982; Mayor Keiser, who
is a California practicing attorney, had his principal office in
Arcadia. On September 7, 1982, he removed himself from that
office and relocated into the office of Mr. Michael Hamud which is
located in Montebello.

The actions by Messrs Crippen, Geronsin and Ingram noted above
were filed one (1) day before the election. Immediately
thereafter Mr. Hamud became the attorney of record for Mayor
Keiser and undertook the defense of these actions and remains his
attorney of record to date.

To ascertain the relationship between himself and Mr. Hamud, I
asked Mayor Keiser for his retainer agreement. He advises me that
there is no retainer agreement; and, that whatever fees were
incurred by Mr. Hamud on his behalf were paid by his insurance
company. His homeowner's policy has picked up the defense of the
action. They are presently retaining a law firm located in San
Bernardino but Mr. Hamud is still one of the attorneys of record.

The Mayor has stated that the only relationship between Mr. Hamud
and himself is as landlord and tenant. I did ask him for a copy
of the Rental Agreement and for cancelled checks; but, he has
advised me that their relationship is based on an oral
understanding and that his rent is paid in cash on the first of
each month. He also stated that no services are performed as part
of the rental payment.

On January 11, 1982 the Mayor appointed Mr. Hamud to the Planning
Commission. The El Monte Municipal Code provides that the Mayor
has the power to appoint with the approval of the Council. Two
other members of the Council voted to approve the appointment.

Ms. Milman
Page 3


At the same time Mr. Hamud was the attorney of record for a Mr.
Ronald Ciner in both criminal and civil matters stemming from the
same incident.  Mr. Ciner had been arrested in 1979 by El Monte
Police officers on criminal charges that are still presently
pending an appellate court decision.  A claim for damages was
filed with the City in 1979 by Mr. Hamud on behalf of Mr. Ciner as
a result of that arrest.

Subsequently, in February 1982 Mr. Hamud filed a Summons and
Complaint as attorney of record for Mr. Ciner naming this City and
its officers as defendants.  However, that Summons and Complaint
was never served on the City or the officers.  Therefore, at the
time of Mr. Hamud's appointment to the Planning Commission it was
unknown to the City Council members that such a lawsuit had been
initiated.  The existence of this lawsuit and Mr. Hamud's
involvement were first brought to the City's attention in June
1983 when Mr. Hamud served the City of El Monte and the three
police officers with the Summons and Complaint.

The El Monte Police Officers Association appeared before the City
Council on June 28, 1983 and questioned the propriety of Mr. Hamud
acting as plaintiff's attorney since he is serving as a Planning
Commissioner of the City of El Monte and is paid $25.00 per
meeting.  There are generally 3 meetings per month, so his
compensation is approximately $900.00 per year plus expenses for a
limited number of conferences.

The questions posed by the Police Association are as follows:

Considering the relationship between Mr. Hamud and Mayor Keiser is
there a conflict problem if Mr. Hamud institutes or continues suit
against the City?  In any tort action the attorney must first
submit a claim which must be approved or rejected by the Council
which includes the Mayor.  Does that raise a conflict?  In
addition, settlements over $5,000 must be approved by the City
Council including the Mayor in closed session.  Does that
constitute a violation?

Considering the relationship of Mr. Hamud to the Mayor, was the
appointment to the Planning Commission a violation?

Considering Mr. Hamud's position in a lawsuit as both the
plaintiff's attorney and the defendant's planning commissioner, is
there a violation?

Ms. Milman
Page 4


I am enclosing a copy of a letter of inquiry that I gave to the
Mayor and the Mayor's letter in response thereto.

Thank you for your prompt attention and response to this matter.

Very truly yours,


Sidney Maleck
City Attorney

SM:JFL:ah
Enclosures

CITY OF EL MONTE

June 29, 1983

Mayor Thomas Keiser
c/o 817 Beverly Blvd.
Suite 103
Montebello, CA 90640

Re: Hamud - Possible Conflict

Dear Mayor Keiser:

At the regular meeting of the City Council of June
28, 1983, the Council directed me to work with the FPPC
and the State Bar to determine whether there are any
conflicts with the appointment of Mr. Hamud to the
Planning Commission and whether Mr. Hamud may properly
litigate cases against the City, the City Council and the
police officers. In order for me to be able to present
all of the facts to the above agencies I would request
that you provide me with the following information:

The exact date when you entered into a lease or sublease
with Mr. Hamud to occupy the premises in Montebello. A
copy of the lease or rental agreement would be appre-
ciated. Do you perform services as part of rental
payment?

The exact date when you retained Mr. Hamud for the
purpose of representing you against Messrs. Crippen,
Gerosin and Ingram. A copy of the retainer agreement
that you executed with Mr. Hamud would be helpful. It
would also be helpful if you could provide us with dates
when payment on that retainer agreement were made and any
cancelled checks to confirm those payments.

Although this question was asked, I would appreciate a
statement from you that Mr. Hamud does not make
appearances on your behalf in any cases and that you do
not make any appearances for Mr. Hamud on any cases and a
statement from you that the only relationship between you
and Mr. Hamud is as a landlord tenant and that Mr. Hamud
is your attorney in the Crippen, Gerosin and Ingram matter
only.

Mayor Keiser
Page 2


I have one other question which I think may be pertinent, did
We Hamad advise you at the time that you appointed him to the
Planning Commission that he had open cases against the City.
Your responses will help to expedite this entire matter.
Thank you.

Very truly yours,

Sidney Maleck
City Attorney

SM:ah

Law Offices
THOMAS KEISER
Suite 103
817 W. Beverly Boulevard
Montebello, California 90640
(213) 726-1154


June 5, 1983


Sidney Maleck
City Attorney
11333 Valley Boulevard
El Monte, CA 91734

Re: Michael F. Hamud

Dear Mr. Maleck:

     In response to your letter addressed to me dated
June 29, 1983, be advised of the following:

     1. I occupied the premises at 817 W. Beverly
Boulevard, Suite 103, Montebello, commencing September 7, 1982.
An oral agreement exists between myself and Michael Hamud
regarding my tenancy. Rent is paid in cash on the first day
of each month. No services are performed as part of the rental
payment;

     2. I consulted Mr. Hamud regarding the Crippen
v. Keiser, Geronsin v. Keiser, and Ingram v. Keiser lawsuits
sometime immediately following my being served. I did not sign
any written retainer agreement with Mr. Hamud. Mr. Hamud was
compensated in full for his services on my behalf by The
Travelers, my homeowner's insurance carrier, after they
assumed my defense; and,

     3. The only relationship between myself and Mr.
Hamud is that of landlord and tenant and Mr. Hamud is one of
my attorneys of record in the Crippen v. Keiser, Geronsin v.
Keiser, and Ingram v. Keiser matters.

     Should either the Fair Political Practices
Commission, the State Bar, or any other agency have need for
further information you may suggest they contact me directly.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
THOMAS KEISER

TK/c

cc: Michael F. Hamud, Esq.

sections.conclusion: We cannot issue advice on Mayor Keiser's or Commissioner Hamud's past conduct. We will refer your letter to our Enforcement Division for their review.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Mayor Thomas Keiser's appointment of attorney Michael Hamud to the Planning Commission constitutes a conflict of interest, and whether Hamud may properly prosecute lawsuits against the City while serving as a Planning Commissioner, given Hamud's prior support for Keiser's election and their business relationship.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to issue advice on the past conduct of Mayor Keiser and Commissioner Hamud, citing its policy against advising on past conduct that may or may not have violated the Political Reform Act, and referred the matter to the Enforcement Division for review.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document contains multiple letters including the incoming request dated July 8, 1983, an acknowledgment letter dated July 13, 1983, and the FPPC response dated July 22, 1983. The FPPC response declines to issue advice on past conduct per established policy. Question was synthesized from the City Attorney's detailed factual recitation and specific questions posed, rather than a formal QUESTION section.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Mayor Thomas Keiser's appointment of attorney Michael Hamud to the Planning Commission constitutes a conflict of interest, and whether Hamud may properly prosecute lawsuits against the City while serving as a Planning Commissioner, given Hamud's prior support for Keiser's election and their business relationship.

CONCLUSION: We cannot issue advice on Mayor Keiser's or Commissioner Hamud's past conduct. We will refer your letter to our Enforcement Division for their review.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of El Monte requested an FPPC opinion regarding potential conflicts of interest arising from Mayor Keiser's appointment of attorney Michael Hamud to the Planning Commission and Hamud's simultaneous prosecution of lawsuits against the City. The FPPC declined to provide advice on past conduct and referred the matter to its Enforcement Division.
